_doc: CANONICAL narrated-cut config (the shipped post-meeting workflow video, 2026-06-02). Copy this for a new video and change: video/out/voice_dir paths, voice_preset, the cues (text + start_ms + boop/boop_ms), and the sfx times. ALWAYS measure each cue's real duration first (see reference/AUTHORING.md) and lay out start_ms with ~1.5s gaps so nothing overlaps. start_ms values here are tuned to the timings baked into reference/post-meeting-workflow.html.

cues.text: Let me show you how this works in practice. The moment I finish a client call, this is what I do.
I've just finished a call with Harvey, one of our newest clients.
I'll just voice note my system, asking it to debrief the meeting. It pulls the transcript automatically, takes the decisions and the action items, and saves the notes.
Then I ask it to build the proposal, in our own branding, from what we just discussed. A couple of minutes later it's ready, and I can edit it right here, inside my operating system.
It even drafts the follow-up email for me, with the proposal attached. I just check it, say yes, and it sends straight to the client.

And the moment it sends, everything updates on its own. The deal moves forward in my CRM, and the next call gets booked. <break time="0.7s" /> What used to take me the whole afternoon, <break time="0.3s" /> done in a couple of minutes.

And all of this happened without me ever switching between tools, or leaving my workspace. It was all done right here, in my AI Operating System. <break time="0.6s" /> And this is just one example. It can run almost any workflow in your business.
